name: Noah Gray
headline: Chiefs blocking TE2 craters without 2024 red-zone luck
body:
Gray's 2025 was a sharp regression. He played 14 games on a 52.1 percent snap share, ran 265 routes, and turned a 6.7 percent target share into just 21 catches for 178 yards and zero touchdowns, good for 17.8 fantasy points and a TE56 finish. That collapse is stark against 2024, when he caught 40 of 49 targets for 437 yards and five scores on a 0.473 EPA per play to land TE29. His efficiency cratered: 0.67 yards per route run versus 1.34, a -0.16 EPA, and a 35.1 percent success rate.

The profile is entirely matchup- and touchdown-dependent behind Travis Kelce, with red-zone work the only ceiling driver. He saw only two red-zone targets in 2025 after 10 in 2024, and three drops on 24 catchable balls did not help. Now 26, Gray is a blocking-first TE2 whose relevance evaporates when the scores dry up. Leave him on the waiver wire in all redraft formats; he is a desperation bye-week streamer at best.
